task_completion.description: Verify the agent opens and reads the grid specification file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, file viewer, or terminal window displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/grid_specification.txt'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent creates a new IFC4 project in Bonsai (BlenderBIM).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender interface is open, and the Bonsai/BlenderBIM scene properties panel shows an active IFC4 project has been initialized.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent creates a structural reference grid (IfcGrid).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender Outliner displays an 'IfcGrid' object, or the viewport shows a structural grid being actively edited using Bonsai tools.

task_completion.description: Verify the grid features four lettered U-axes (A, B, C, D).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The grid configuration panel or the 3D viewport clearly shows four axes labeled A, B, C, and D.

task_completion.description: Verify the grid features three numbered V-axes (1, 2, 3).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The grid configuration panel or the 3D viewport clearly shows three axes labeled 1, 2, and 3 intersecting the U-axes.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent saves the IFC model to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Blender export/save dialog showing the path '/home/ga/BIMProjects/warehouse_grid.ifc', or an info message/terminal output confirming the project was saved to this exact path.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Blender/Bonsai GUI to create the grid.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through Blender menus, using the Bonsai add-on panels, and interacting with the 3D viewport, rather than solely executing a Python script in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the grid is a genuine IFC element and not a visual fake.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Outliner should show proper IFC hierarchy (e.g., IfcProject -> IfcSite -> IfcBuilding -> IfcGrid) rather than standard Blender mesh lines and text objects manually arranged to look like a grid.

integrity.description: Verify the output file was generated by the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file '/home/ga/BIMProjects/warehouse_grid.ifc' should be saved via Bonsai's 'Save Project' or 'Export IFC' functionality, not created using terminal commands like 'touch' or 'cp'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The specification document is located at /home/ga/Desktop/grid_specification.txt. The structural grid must contain exactly 4 U-Axes (A, B, C, D) and 3 V-Axes (1, 2, 3). The final IFC model must be saved to /home/ga/BIMProjects/warehouse_grid.ifc.
